Keychain.title: Keychain
Keychain.description: Represents a premium keychain product available for display on the landing page and for sale in the store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Keychain entity.

name.description: The name of the keychain product.

description.description: A brief description highlighting the key features or aesthetic of the keychain.

imageUrl.description: The URL to the main image of the keychain, suitable for product cards and showcases.

price.description: The selling price of the keychain.

storeLink.description: The URL to the external store page where this keychain can be purchased.

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating whether this keychain should be highlighted in the 'Featured Keychains' section on the landing page.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the keychain record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the keychain record was last updated.

GalleryImage.title: GalleryImage
GalleryImage.description: Represents an image used in the lifestyle gallery section, showcasing keychains in real-life contexts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GalleryImage entity.

imageUrl.description: The URL to the lifestyle image.

caption.description: A short descriptive text or context for the image.

keychainId.description: Reference to the Keychain entity that is featured in this image. (Relationship: Keychain 1:N GalleryImage)

displayOrder.description: An optional numerical value to control the display order of images within the gallery.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the gallery image record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the gallery image record was last updated.

CustomKeychainRequest.title: CustomKeychainRequest
CustomKeychainRequest.description: Represents a customer's request for a custom-designed keychain, including their specifications and an optional reference image.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomKeychainRequest entity.

customerName.description: The full name of the customer submitting the request.

customerEmail.description: The email address of the customer for communication regarding their request.

requestDetails.description: A detailed textual description provided by the customer outlining their custom keychain design preferences.

submittedImageUrl.description: The URL to an image uploaded by the customer, serving as a reference or example for their custom keychain design. This property is optional.

requestDate.description: The date and time when the custom keychain request was submitted.

status.description: The current processing status of the custom keychain request (e.g., 'Pending', 'Reviewed', 'In Progress', 'Completed', 'Rejected').

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the custom keychain request record was last updated.

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection of all premium keychains. Used for product displays, featured sections, and linking to external stores. All documents in this collection are public.

Publicly accessible collection of lifestyle images showcasing keychains in real-life contexts. References 'keychainId' but is a top-level collection for simpler public access. All documents in this collection are public.

Stores customer requests for custom keychains, owned by the submitting user. Each document will include a denormalized 'ownerId' field that matches the '{userId}' wildcard from the path, ensuring explicit authorization. Only the 'userId' in the path (i.e., 'request.auth.uid') can create, read, or update their own requests.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user submitting the custom keychain request.

params.name: customKeychainRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific custom keychain request.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Public Collections (`/keychains`, `/galleryImages`):** These collections hold data that is globally visible and does not require user-specific authorization. Therefore, no `get()` calls are needed in security rules, ensuring atomic operations and full authorization independence. All documents are directly accessible based on their path.
2.  **User-Owned Data (`/users/{userId}/customKeychainRequests/{customKeychainRequestId}`):** For custom keychain requests, authorization is primarily tied to the user submitting the request. To achieve authorization independence, each `CustomKeychainRequest` document will include a denormalized `ownerId` field. This `ownerId` must match the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This allows security rules to validate ownership directly from the document itself (`resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid`) without needing to traverse parent documents via `get()`, thus enabling atomic operations for creating, updating, and deleting requests.

**Query-Ability for Rules (QAPs):**
1.  **Public Collections:** By placing `Keychains` and `GalleryImages` in top-level collections (`/keychains`, `/galleryImages`), we ensure a homogeneous security posture: all documents within these collections are publicly readable. This inherently supports QAPs for `list` operations, as clients can query and retrieve all items without needing complex, filtering security rules.
2.  **User-Owned Data:** The hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/customKeychainRequests/{customKeychainRequestId}` is crucial for QAPs. It naturally scopes `list` operations to the authenticated user's own requests. A security rule like `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;` ensures that when a user queries `/users/myUserId/customKeychainRequests`, only *their* requests are returned, and the rule's conditions are applied efficiently at the path level, avoiding the need for database filters in rules.

**Structural Segregation and Access Modeling:**
*   `Keychains` and `GalleryImages` are segregated into their own top-level collections because they share a public security posture.
*   `CustomKeychainRequest` data is segregated under `/users/{userId}/` because it has a private, user-specific security posture.

This design prioritizes explicit ownership, minimizes rule complexity, and ensures that `list` operations are secure and performant by leveraging the structure itself rather than relying on inefficient rule-based filtering or cross-document lookups.
